# apim_dictionary (Resource)

Dictionary Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "apim_dictionary" "my_dictionary" {
  deployed    = false
  description = "...my_description..."
  dynamic = {
    provider = {
      http = {
        body = "...my_body..."
        headers = [
          {
            name  = "...my_name..."
            value = "...my_value..."
          }
        ]
        method           = "CONNECT"
        specification    = "...my_specification..."
        type             = "HTTP"
        url              = "https://cautious-futon.com/"
        use_system_proxy = false
      }
    }
    trigger = {
      rate = 4
      unit = "MILLISECONDS"
    }
  }
  environment_id = "a44e0d1b-9fa9-4d64-8b76-3634623a2e27"
  hrid           = "demo_api"
  manual = {
    properties = {
      key = "value"
    }
  }
  name            = "...my_name..."
  organization_id = "dedd0e0f-b3e9-4d2f-89cd-b2a9de7cb145"
  type            = "DYNAMIC"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `deployed` (Boolean) If true, a `MANUAL` type dictionary is deployed in the gateway, a `DYNAMIC` type dictionary is started. 
Changing this value back to false will stop the dictionary or undeploy it.
- `hrid` (String) A unique human readable id identifying this resource. Requires replacement if changed.
- `name` (String) Display name of the dictionary
- `type` (String) Type of dictionary. MANUAL is to be updated manually, DYNAMIC is updated and deployed automatically. must be one of ["MANUAL", "DYNAMIC"]

### Optional

- `description` (String) Detailed description of the dictionary
- `dynamic` (Attributes) A dynamic dictionary populated from an external provider on a schedule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--dynamic))
- `environment_id` (String) environment ID
- `manual` (Attributes) A manual dictionary with static key/value properties. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--manual))
- `organization_id` (String) organization ID

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

<a id="nestedatt--dynamic"></a>
### Nested Schema for `dynamic`

Required:

- `provider` (Attributes) Provider of a 'DYNAMIC' type dictionary (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--dynamic--provider))
- `trigger` (Attributes) Renewal configuration for a 'DYNAMIC' dictionary (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--dynamic--trigger))

<a id="nestedatt--dynamic--provider"></a>
### Nested Schema for `dynamic.provider`

Optional:

- `http` (Attributes) HTTP dictionary provider configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--dynamic--provider--http))

<a id="nestedatt--dynamic--provider--http"></a>
### Nested Schema for `dynamic.provider.http`

Optional:

- `body` (String) Optional request payload
- `headers` (Attributes List) HTTP Headers (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--dynamic--provider--http--headers))
- `method` (String) Not Null; must be one of ["GET", "POST", "PUT", "PATCH", "DELETE", "HEAD", "OPTIONS", "TRACE", "CONNECT"]
- `specification` (String) Transformation of the returned payload (JOLT Specification). Not Null
- `type` (String) Type of dictionary provider. Not Null; must be "HTTP"
- `url` (String, Sensitive) URL of the provider. Not Null
- `use_system_proxy` (Boolean)

<a id="nestedatt--dynamic--provider--http--headers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `dynamic.provider.http.headers`

Optional:

- `name` (String) Header name. Not Null
- `value` (String) Header value. Not Null




<a id="nestedatt--dynamic--trigger"></a>
### Nested Schema for `dynamic.trigger`

Required:

- `rate` (Number)
- `unit` (String) must be one of ["MICROSECONDS", "MILLISECONDS", "SECONDS", "MINUTES", "HOURS", "DAYS"]



<a id="nestedatt--manual"></a>
### Nested Schema for `manual`

Required:

- `properties` (Map of String) Dictionary data are key/value pairs for `MANUAL` properties

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = apim_dictionary.my_apim_dictionary
  id = jsonencode({
    environment_id  = "a44e0d1b-9fa9-4d64-8b76-3634623a2e27"
    hrid            = "my_demo_api"
    organization_id = "dedd0e0f-b3e9-4d2f-89cd-b2a9de7cb145"
  })
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import apim_dictionary.my_apim_dictionary '{"environment_id": "a44e0d1b-9fa9-4d64-8b76-3634623a2e27", "hrid": "my_demo_api", "organization_id": "dedd0e0f-b3e9-4d2f-89cd-b2a9de7cb145"}'
```
